course_objectives: To provide knowledge of analytical geometry, multivariable calculus, ordinary differential equations, and integral transforms applicable to engineering problems.

topics.topic: 1.1 Plane — equation through a point, angle between planes, distance from a point
topics.note: Plane through a given line, image of a point in a plane, angle between planes. Asked in almost every paper.

topics.topic: 1.2 Straight Lines — symmetrical form, shortest distance between skew lines
topics.note: Shortest distance between skew lines is the most frequently asked geometry topic. The same pair of lines repeat across multiple papers.

topics.topic: 1.3 Sphere — standard and general equation, great circle, touching a plane
topics.note: Sphere through a circle touching a given plane: Asked in EVERY paper. x²+y²+z²=1, 2x+4y+5z=6 touching z=0 is a recurring problem.

topics.topic: 1.4 Cone and Cylinder — general equation, right circular cylinder
topics.note: Cone and cylinder appear mainly as short notes in Q7.

topics.topic: 2.1 Partial Derivatives — first and second order, mixed partial derivatives
topics.note: Mixed partials (showing f_xy = f_yx or ≠ f_yx) asked in multiple papers.
Asked in EVERY paper without exception. Most common: u=sin⁻¹((x+y)/(√x+√y)) or u=cos⁻¹((x+y)/(√x+√y)) applications.

topics.topic: 2.3 Maxima and Minima of Functions of Two/Three Variables
topics.note: Lagrange multipliers method. Common: optimize x²+y²+z² subject to constraint. Box optimization also frequent.

topics.topic: 3.1 Double Integrals — Cartesian and polar, change of order
topics.note: Change of order of integration appears in EVERY paper. Very high frequency topic.
Volume in first octant bounded by cylinder and plane is extremely frequent.

topics.topic: 3.3 Change of Variables — polar, cylindrical, spherical coordinates
topics.note: ∫∫(x²−y²)/(x²+y²) over a region changing to polar is a frequently repeated problem.

topics.topic: 4.1 Exact Differential Equations and Integrating Factors
topics.note: State condition for exactness and solve. x²ydx−(x³+y³)dy=0 type appears repeatedly.
Very frequently asked. dy/dx + (1/x)sin2y = x³cos²y is the most repeated Bernoulli problem.
Integrating factor method, solutions with initial conditions.
dy/dx − tany/(1+x) = (1+x)eˣsecy type: appears in 2021F, 2019F, 2018F, 2017F, 2017Sp.

topics.topic: 5.1 Homogeneous Linear ODE with Constant Coefficients
topics.note: Complementary function problems with initial conditions.
y''+9y=6cos3x and similar problems appear frequently.
y''−2y'+y=12eˣ/x³ is the most repeated variation of parameters problem. Appears in at least 6 papers.
x²y''−4xy'+6y=21x⁻⁴ type equations.

course_contents.title: Power Series Solutions and Special Functions

topics.note: Asked in EVERY paper. y''+4y=0, y''-4y=0 are the most commonly asked power series problems.
d/dx[xⁿJₙ(x)] = xⁿJₙ₋₁(x) proof appears as OR alternative to power series in every paper.
Less frequent — mainly in 2015F as OR alternative.
L{sin at cos at}, L{t²cos wt}, L{t²sin wt} appear very frequently.
Second shifting theorem and unit step function problems appear often.
L⁻¹{w/[s²(s²+w²)]} and L⁻¹{1/[s²(s²+w²)]} appear very frequently.
Prove convolution theorem and apply it to find inverse transforms.

topics.topic: 7.5 Solution of IVP Using Laplace Transform
topics.note: Solving 2nd order ODE IVPs with Laplace transform appears in EVERY paper as Q6b.

references: 1. Erwin Kreyszig, Advanced Engineering Mathematics, John Wiley & Sons, 10th Edition.
2. B.S. Grewal, Higher Engineering Mathematics, Khanna Publishers.
3. E.D. Rainville and P.E. Bedient, Elementary Differential Equations, Macmillan.
